mission.title: Install and Configure Kubeflow
mission.description: Kubeflow is a comprehensive machine learning toolkit designed for Kubernetes, enabling users to build, deploy, and manage machine learning workflows. It is particularly useful for AI practitioners and platform administrators looking for a modular and scalable solution. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and the need for Kubernetes expertise.

steps.title: Install the Kubeflow CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Kubeflow command-line interface (CLI) which is essential for managing your Kubeflow deployment.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/kubeflow/kubeflow/releases/download/v1.10.0/kubeflow-cli-linux-amd64.tar.gz

tar -xzf kubeflow-cli-linux-amd64.tar.gz

sudo mv kubeflow /usr/local/bin/

```

> Download the Kubeflow CLI for Linux.
> Extract the downloaded tarball.
> Move the CLI to a directory in your PATH.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster on Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create kubeflow-cluster --num-nodes=3 --zone=us-central1-a --cluster-version=1.30

```

> Create a GKE cluster with 3 nodes.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your local kubeconfig file to connect to the newly created Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials kubeflow-cluster --zone=us-central1-a

```

> Fetch cluster credentials.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks that the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster are running and ready.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> List all nodes in the cluster.

steps.title: Install Kubeflow
steps.description:
This step installs Kubeflow using the CLI, deploying the necessary components to your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

kfctl apply -V -f https://github.com/kubeflow/manifests/archive/v1.10.0.tar.gz

```

> Deploy Kubeflow with the specified version.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step configures autoscaling and monitoring for your Kubeflow deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubeflow/manifests/v1.10.0/monitoring/monitoring.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubeflow/manifests/v1.10.0/autoscaling/autoscaling.yaml

```

> Set up monitoring.
> Enable autoscaling.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Kubeflow
uninstall.description:
This step removes Kubeflow from your cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated resources and may lead to data loss.

```bash

kfctl delete -V -f https://github.com/kubeflow/manifests/archive/v1.10.0.tar.gz

gcloud container clusters delete kubeflow-cluster --zone=us-central1-a --quiet

```

> Remove Kubeflow components.
> Delete the GKE cluster.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kubeflow
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data. This step will upgrade your Kubeflow installation to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get all --all-namespaces > kubeflow-backup.yaml

kfctl apply -V -f https://github.com/kubeflow/manifests/archive/v1.10.0.tar.gz

kubectl get pods -n kubeflow-user

```

> Backup current resources.
> Upgrade to the latest version.
> Verify that all pods are running.

troubleshooting.title: kubectl get nodes shows 'NotReady'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Nodes may not have enough resources or are not properly configured.

**Fix:**
Check node resource allocation and ensure nodes are in the correct zone.

troubleshooting.title: Kubeflow dashboard is not accessible.
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Ingress may not be configured correctly.

**Fix:**
Verify ingress settings and ensure the service is exposed.

troubleshooting.title: Pods in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state.
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Application containers may be failing to start due to misconfiguration.

**Fix:**
Check pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' for errors.

troubleshooting.title: kubectl apply fails with 'context deadline exceeded'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Cluster may be under heavy load or network issues.

**Fix:**
Check cluster resource usage and network connectivity.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new ML frameworks, improved integration with Kubernetes 1.30.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated support for Kubernetes versions below 1.27.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.9, ensure all custom resources are compatible with the new version.

resolution.summary: You should see the Kubeflow dashboard accessible at http://<your-cluster-ip>:8080, and all components should be running with 'kubectl get pods -n kubeflow-user'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a GKE account and the necessary permissions to create clusters.
